description:
You are a build-and-release engineer who has just received a freshly-produced binary
artifact that must be delivered to QA.  
Before shipping anything, company policy requires that every artifact be accompanied by:

1. A file named “SHA256SUMS” that lists the SHA-256 checksum of the artifact, in the **exact** format produced by the `sha256sum` tool (checksum, two spaces, filename, and a newline).
2. A human-readable verification log named “checksum_verification.log” that shows the result
   of validating the checksum with `sha256sum -c`.  
   The log must contain exactly the single line returned by `sha256sum -c`,
   followed by a terminating newline.

The artefact has already been placed for you in:

/home/user/builds/releases/hello-1.0.0.bin

Your tasks:

A. Create /home/user/builds/releases/SHA256SUMS that contains **one** line, the SHA-256
   checksum of hello-1.0.0.bin in the canonical `sha256sum` output layout:

<64-hex-digits><two spaces>hello-1.0.0.bin\n

B. Verify the checksum using the `-c` option and capture the console output into
   /home/user/builds/releases/checksum_verification.log so that the file contains
   one exact line:

hello-1.0.0.bin: OK\n

Hints:
• Everything can be completed with about two or three standard `sha256sum` commands and I/O redirection.
• Do **not** hard-code the checksum; compute it from the file itself.
• Make sure both new files end with a single newline and contain no extra spaces or lines.
• You do not need any elevated privileges. Operate entirely inside /home/user/builds/releases.

When you are finished the directory /home/user/builds/releases should contain exactly these three files:

hello-1.0.0.bin
SHA256SUMS
checksum_verification.log

with the two new files having the precise formats described above. The automated tests will fail if there are
additional lines, missing newlines, or any deviation from the expected content.
truth:
Initial state before the agent starts:

Directory:
/home/user/builds/releases  – already exists and is writable by the regular user.

Pre-existing file:
/home/user/builds/releases/hello-1.0.0.bin  
  • Size: 5 bytes  
  • Contents (hex): 68 65 6c 6c 6f  
    (ASCII text “hello” with **no** trailing newline)

Ground-truth checksum that the tests will expect to see in SHA256SUMS:
2cf24dba5fb0a30e26e83b2ac5b9e29e1b161e5c1fa7425e73043362938b9824

Ground-truth required contents of the files after the agent finishes:

/home/user/builds/releases/SHA256SUMS  
  2cf24dba5fb0a30e26e83b2ac5b9e29e1b161e5c1fa7425e73043362938b9824  hello-1.0.0.bin\n

/home/user/builds/releases/checksum_verification.log  
  hello-1.0.0.bin: OK\n
